_comment: RK3566 / Radxa3e aarch64 ground station — PASSIVE video receiver. Single card wlx84fc1450bcde, RX-only: decodes the vehicle's video downlink (link 207) to 127.0.0.1:5600. NO uplink TX, NO probe RX, NO back-channel stats_out — this node never transmits and does not participate in the control link. Channel 161 HT40+ (40 MHz): a passive RX on a 40 MHz channel decodes both 20 MHz and 40 MHz transmissions on the primary, so it works with any vehicle width (vehicle stays HT20 — rtl8812eu has no HT40 monitor mode). UNVERIFIED on this card: HT40 monitor RX was bench-confirmed only on the dev host's RTL88x2 adapters; cold-boot-verify wlx84fc1450bcde decodes cleanly at HT40 before relying on it (fall back to bare 'set channel 161' = HT20 if not). A passive RX does not follow CSA hops, so re-tune by hand if the link hops.

up: iw dev wlx84fc1450bcde set monitor otherbss
iw dev wlx84fc1450bcde set channel 161 HT40+

tunnels._comment: Video downlink RX (single card). link_id 207 / radio_port 0 match the vehicle's video wfb_tx; -x and -l 100 match the active GS video tunnel. udp_out omitted -> wfb_rx default 127.0.0.1:5600 (local decoder/pixelpilot). No stats_out: passive, no uplink to forward to — gs_supervisor still wires -Y into its own local listener, so RSSI/MCS show in the WebUI.
tunnels.name: video
